---
title: "Publication Studio"
subtitle: "Choose one source of truth, then render and publish with confidence"
toc: true
---

## The Quarto publishing model

Every publication begins as a plain-text `.qmd` source file. That source can
contain narrative, citations, equations, figures, tables, diagrams and executable
code. Quarto turns it into the formats the audience needs, without maintaining
separate Word, PDF and website versions.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Default rule
Use **HTML** for a living, interactive or web-first publication; use **PDF** for
a typeset, fixed-layout record; use **DOCX** when collaborators or a journal
need an editable Word document.
:::

## Pick the right starting point

| Goal | Start with | Render command | Best use |
|---|---|---|---|
| Scientific paper / report | `outputs/manuscript.qmd` | `quarto render outputs/manuscript.qmd --to pdf` | One source to HTML, PDF and Word |
| Teaching handout / clinical explainer | `outputs/interactive.qmd` | `quarto render outputs/interactive.qmd` | Browser-based interaction with no server |
| Conference talk | `outputs/slides.qmd` | `quarto render outputs/slides.qmd --to revealjs` | Reveal.js, speaker notes and PDF export |
| Website / knowledge hub | project root | `quarto render` | Linked, searchable, versioned content |
| Handbook / course | `book/` | `quarto render book --to pdf` | Multi-chapter HTML and PDF |
| Fresh manuscript | `templates/manuscript-starter.qmd` | Copy it into `outputs/` first | A clean, publication-safe scaffold |

## Authoring standards

### Scientific Markdown

- Put title, authorship, abstract, keywords, bibliography and output choices in
  the YAML front matter at the top of a `.qmd` file.
- Cite with `[@citation-key]`; use `@fig-label`, `@tbl-label` and `@sec-label`
  to cross-reference material that may move while you edit.
- Give every figure and table a descriptive caption and explain its clinical or
  scientific meaning in the surrounding text.
- Treat `references.bib` as a data source, not a source of truth: verify every
  DOI, PMID, author list and journal record before publishing.

### Dynamic and interactive content

Choose the execution engine by the work being done, not by the output format:

- **Python / Jupyter** for scientific Python, notebooks and analysis pipelines.
- **R / knitr** for R statistics, `ggplot2`, tables and established R workflows.
- **Julia / IJulia** for numerical or performance-intensive work.
- **Observable JS** for lightweight, client-side interactivity; the reader's
  browser runs it, so no application server is required.

The engine examples in this site are executable smoke tests, not just snippets.

## Reliable daily workflow

```bash
# Start a fresh paper; the relative bibliography path is then correct.
cp templates/manuscript-starter.qmd outputs/my-study.qmd

# Work interactively; the browser refreshes whenever you save.
quarto preview

# Rebuild the public site, executing changed computational documents.
quarto render

# Render an individual publication in the deliverable format required.
quarto render outputs/manuscript.qmd --to pdf
quarto render outputs/manuscript.qmd --to docx
quarto render outputs/slides.qmd --to revealjs
quarto render book --to pdf
```

`freeze: auto` is enabled for the website. Quarto records the computed results
under `_freeze/`, so a repeat render and a future GitHub Pages build can reuse
the evidence-generating computation rather than silently rerunning it in a
different environment.

## Publication gate

Before distributing a scientific output, check the following:

1. **Evidence** — citations and identifiers have been retrieved and verified in
   the current project, and any clinical claim is appropriate for its audience.
2. **Reproducibility** — render from a clean session; inspect figures, tables,
   labels and numerical values in the target format.
3. **Accessibility** — use informative headings, alt text for meaningful images,
   readable colour contrast, and a table summary where needed.
4. **Audience output** — inspect the actual PDF, DOCX, slides or deployed page;
   a successful command alone is not editorial approval.

## Publishing a website

When this folder lives in its own Git repository, use GitHub Pages or Netlify as
the deployment target. The included `.gitignore` excludes rendered artefacts but
keeps `_freeze/` available for reproducible continuous publishing.

```bash
# First publish to a GitHub repository with Pages enabled.
quarto publish gh-pages
```

Quarto creates and publishes a `gh-pages` branch for a typical project site.
For a custom domain or Netlify site, configure that hosting target once and keep
the source and publication history in the same repository.

## Further reference

The official [Quarto guide](https://quarto.org/docs/guide/) is the primary
reference. The next most useful sections are [citations](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/citations.html),
[books](https://quarto.org/docs/books/), [manuscripts](https://quarto.org/docs/manuscripts/),
[Observable JS](https://quarto.org/docs/interactive/ojs/) and
[GitHub Pages publishing](https://quarto.org/docs/publishing/github-pages.html).
